title: File Explorer Deep Dive Features
subtitle: organize, search, manage!
overview: This session helps students understand File Explorer in Windows, including file organization, folder management, search tools, copy-paste operations, shortcuts, and storage management using real-life examples.

keyTopics: What is File Explorer and why it is important
Sorting files by name, size and date
Using Recycle Bin to restore deleted files
Understanding drives — C Drive, D Drive, USB Drive

activity.title: Create and Organize School Folder Activity
steps: Open File Explorer using Windows + E shortcut
Create a new folder named 'School Work'
Inside it, create folders for Math, Science and English
Create a text file inside each folder
Copy one file to another folder
Delete a file and restore it from Recycle Bin
Search for a file using the search bar

quiz.q: What is File Explorer mainly used for?
quiz.explanation: File Explorer helps users organize, view, copy, move and manage files and folders on a computer.

quiz.q: Which shortcut key opens File Explorer?
quiz.explanation: Windows + E is the shortcut key used to quickly open File Explorer.

quiz.q: A folder is mainly used to:
quiz.explanation: Folders help keep files organized properly.

quiz.q: Which option is used to duplicate a file?
quiz.explanation: Copy creates another version of the same file.

quiz.q: What happens when a file is deleted normally?
quiz.explanation: Deleted files usually move to Recycle Bin first.

quiz.q: Which feature helps find files quickly?
quiz.explanation: The Search Bar in File Explorer helps locate files and folders quickly.

quiz.q: Which storage device can be connected externally?
quiz.explanation: USB drives are external storage devices.
Ctrl + C copies selected files or text.
Ctrl + V pastes the copied or cut item.

quiz.q: Which option changes the name of a file?
quiz.explanation: Rename allows users to change the file or folder name.

quiz.q: Which drive usually contains Windows operating system files?
quiz.explanation: The C Drive generally stores Windows system files.

quiz.q: What information can be seen in file properties?
quiz.explanation: File properties show details like size, type and creation date.

options: Moves a file from one place to another
quiz.explanation: Cut removes the file from its current location and moves it after paste.

quiz.q: Which view helps display files in small picture previews?
quiz.explanation: Icons View displays file previews as icons or thumbnails.

quiz.q: Why is organizing files into folders important?
options: To find files easily and keep data organized
quiz.explanation: Proper folder organization helps users quickly locate and manage files.
